constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: The #initialize method creates a new image object. The image object will have no data if a path to the image is not provided.
constructors.remarks: The #initialize method creates a new image object. The image object will have no data if a path to the image is not provided.

throws.when: if the file path or image is invalid.

methods.name: bits_per_pixel
methods.summary: The #bits_per_pixel method gets the number of bits per pixel in the image.
methods.remarks: The #bits_per_pixel method gets the number of bits per pixel in the image.

methods.name: color_at_uv
methods.summary: The #color_at_uv method returns a color corresponding to the UV texture coordinates.
methods.remarks: The #color_at_uv method returns a color corresponding to the UV texture coordinates. 0.0, 0.0 maps to the bottom left and 1.0, 1.0 to the top right of the image.

params.name: bilinear
params.doc: Use bilinear texture filtering. This interpolates the colors instead of picking the nearest neighbor.

methods.name: colors
methods.summary: The #colors method returns an array of Color for each pixel in the image.
methods.remarks: The #colors method returns an array of Color for each pixel in the image.

methods.name: data
methods.summary: Note: The byte order of the pixels are RGB(A) on macOS and BGR(A) on Windows.
methods.remarks: Note: The byte order of the pixels are RGB(A) on macOS and BGR(A) on Windows. The #data method gets the pixel data for an image in a string of bytes.

methods.name: height
methods.summary: The #height method returns the height of an image.
methods.remarks: The #height method returns the height of an image.

methods.name: load_file
methods.summary: The #load_file method loads image data from the specified file.
methods.remarks: The #load_file method loads image data from the specified file.

throws.when: if the filepath or image is invalid.

methods.name: row_padding
methods.summary: The #row_padding method returns the size of the row padding of an image in bytes.
methods.remarks: The #row_padding method returns the size of the row padding of an image in bytes.

methods.name: save_file
methods.summary: The #save_file method saves an image data object to an image file specified by a path.
methods.remarks: The #save_file method saves an image data object to an image file specified by a path.

methods.name: set_data
methods.signature: set_data(width, height, bits_per_pixel, row_padding, pixel_data) => Sketchup::ImageRep
methods.summary: Note: The byte order of the pixels are RGB(A) on macOS and BGR(A) on Windows.
methods.remarks: Note: The byte order of the pixels are RGB(A) on macOS and BGR(A) on Windows. Note: The encoding of the pixel_data String parameter should be ASCII-8BIT. Any other encoding could corrupt the binary data. Using Array#pack(“C*”) gives correct encoding. The #set_data method discards any existing data and sets new pixel data for the Sketchup::ImageRep.

params.name: width
params.doc: The width of the pixel data. Must be greater than 0.

params.name: height
params.doc: The height of the pixel data. Must be greater than 0.

params.name: bits_per_pixel
params.doc: The bits per pixel for the pixel data. Must be either 8/24/32.

params.name: row_padding
params.doc: The row padding for the pixel data which is sized in bytes. Row padding is used to pad each row with zeros so that each scanline on the pixel data will end on the data-type boundary.

params.name: pixel_data
params.doc: The binary string containing the pixel data representing the new image.

throws.when: If width, height, bits_per_pixel or pixel_data are invalid.
If width, height, bits_per_pixel or pixel_data are wrong data types.

methods.name: size
methods.summary: The #size method gets the total size of the image data in bytes.
methods.remarks: The #size method gets the total size of the image data in bytes.

methods.name: width
methods.summary: The #width method returns the width of an image.
methods.remarks: The #width method returns the width of an image.
